characters.name: Adele
characters.description: AdeleisFelix'sbiological half-sister who debuts in "The Stigmata of Progress". She shares the same father as Felix, however they have different mothers. She is portrayed by Lauren Hammersley. Shameless, brazen, and spontaneous, Adele is a wickedly intelligent lawyer who outwits even when heavily intoxicated — and that’s often. She made her way through law school relying on the laurels of her IQ. With her practice suspended due to her showing up to court intoxicated, her benders have grown to epic proportions. Regardless of her activities, she presents in high-fashion corporate wear — a somewhat jarring juxtaposition to her crude brand of humor.[1]Adele and Felix found each other through the program "GeneConnexions". Adele is American (unlike her brother) and her accent indicates that she is from the South. She has caramel brown eyes and medium-long strawberry blond hair. She is tall and of slim build.

characters.name: Alan Nealon
characters.description: Dr. Alan Nealonis a doctor/surgeon and geneticist working for Neolution. He is sent to infiltrate theDyad Institute, where he is in charge ofCosima's, and laterRachel's, recovery. Though he is not actually seen, it is Dr. Nealon that has examinedSarah(while she was impersonatingBeth) in her sleep. Dr. Nealon debuts in theseason 2finale, "By Means Which Have Never Yet Been Tried". He gets Sarah to sign her consent on medical procedures/practices in the future. Not fully reading the consent form, Sarah signs hastily while wishing to be taken toKiraimmediately. Later in the episode, he is seen prepping for surgery as he informs Sarah that she is about to have anoophorectomy.

characters.name: Aldous Leekie
characters.description:
Dr.Aldous Leekiewas a scientist involved with the handling of theclones. Being the "face" ofNeolution, he was a provocative and widely read "pop scientist", author and speaker. He was the de-facto primary antagonist ofSeason 1. His research center, theDyad Institute, pushes the scientific limits of what is possible – as well as legal and ethical. Here, he seems to be in the process of conducting experiments to support their "Neolutionist" theories, or self-directed evolution through modern implants. When asked what his "ideal human" would look like, he jokingly suggested "people with white hair and one white eye". Though considered 'fringe' by the scientific establishment, his philosophy of self-directed evolution attracts not only freaks and 'futurists', but healthy private investment. Avid followers of Leekie's teachings who have adopted this appearance have been dubbed "Freaky Leekies" by the media. Ethan Duncan claims Aldous Leekie was responsible for the fire that destroyed the original genome the female clones were derived from and killed several Project Leda scientists and seemly Ethan's wifeSusan Duncan. Aldous however claims he killed Susan because of the Duncans' salting the petri dishes and attempting to escape with her.
Dr. Leekie was known to have worked withDelphine Cormierfor an unspecified project. Although the specifics of their relationship are still unknown, they seem to be in an intimate one.

characters.name: Alison Hendrix
characters.description: 38(Audio Series) Leda clones(sisters/genetic identicals)Castor clones(genetic brothers)Kendall Malone(sister/genetic template)Kira Manning(genetic niece; viaSarah)Siobhan Sadler(genetic niece; viaKendall)Donnie(genetic nephew; viaHelena)Arthur(genetic nephew; viaHelena)Gabriele(genetic niece; viaCosima(audio series) Alison Hendrixis acloneof Canadian origin and one of the four main protagonists onOrphan Black. Her "tag number" is327B24. Alison is an uptight suburbanite, a devoted mother and wife, with a typical marriage and family and a single minded drive to maintain the status quo. As Alison tends to put her family ahead of herself and is the most rooted of the clones, the new reality is hard on her because unlike Cosima and Beth, she also fears for the safety of her children. She becomes paranoid, and starts suspecting her seemingly innocent husband Donnie of being her monitor, but in the end, with the help of Sarah, snaps out of it finding the necessary strength to go forward. Alison’s instability goes as far to her having a drug addiction, relying on pills to get her by. She is also easily shaken, as Sarah angrily smacked her for pointing a gun at Felix, an act that left Alison visibly distraught, walking off holding her face while crying and shaking. In Season 5, Alison embraces her impulses. She becomes much more like her sisters, taking an interest in music; though her sweetness is retained. She also finally calls out the hipocracy of her neighbors who ganged up on her for her faults. Alison also finally confronts her sister, Rachel Duncan, and demands her to call off Enger (who was digging up the bodies she and Donnie had buried) after presenting the head of her former boss, Aldous Leekie. Alison is initially sceptical to explore her nature as a clone, and form connections with her sisters, being hesitant to even call them her family. She eventually becomes a big part in the mission to find out more about who created them and how to deal with enemies to the LEDAs, and forms a sisterly bond with Clone Club. After mending her marriage, a sexual side is revealed, as Donnie and Alison are seen many times aggressively partaking in sexual acts together.

characters.name: Amelia
characters.description: Ameliais the birth mother ofSarah ManningandHelena. Upon moving to London, 22-year-old Amelia was approached by two scientists, then posing as a couple, to be the in-vitro surrogate of their child. In exchange for money and help to settle into the country, Amelia accepted. Eventually, though, Amelia became suspicious and, realizing that the life inside of her was in danger, ran. She gave birth to twins while in hiding and, knowing that she could not keep them, gave them away: Sarah to the state as a ward, and Helena to the church although Helena was moved to Ukraine. 28 years later,Mrs. S, upon questions from Sarah about her past, asks her contacts from London about where they got Sarah. Amelia apparently heard that Sarah was looking for her, so she immediately traveled to Canada to see her. She proceeds to tell Mrs. S and Sarah about her story, consequently stopping Sarah from handing Helena over toLeekie. Sarah then introduces her to a tied-up Helena, and their reunion is cut short when the police department come for Sarah and interrogate them.

characters.name: Angela Deangelis
characters.description: Angela "Angie" Deangelisis a detective who works alongsideArthur Belland worked alongsideBeth Childs. She is the one that never seems to get what she wants, which is Beth’s partner, Art. She is gunning for Beth's partner because he is really high up in the precinct. She is a bit of a keener and very by the textbook. But this soon changed, and she found herself getting more out of it than she wanted. She is good at what she does and is very tough.[1] Detective Angela Deangelis is a coworker of Art and Beth in the precinct. She is among the few people who greetsSarah, while posing as Beth, when she shows up at the precinct. Angela is a tough and slightly sadistic woman. She acts very intimidating towards everyone around her and is not afraid of acting on her own against her partner's wishes. She enjoys the sight of actual corpses, which is, unlike her fearlessness and toughness, an unusual trait for a policewoman. Despite all that, she overlooks obvious things. Primarily the fact Beth, Sarah and Alison are clones; even Donnie called her stupid (and THAT'S saying something.)

characters.name: Anita Bowers
characters.description: Dr.Anita Bowersis the psychiatric doctor ofBeth Childs. More than once, Beth had requested extra prescriptions, most of which Dr. Bowers seem to have agreed to as Beth has a whole cupboard of her drugs. Sarah Manningfirst meets the doctor in "Natural Selection" when Sarah, as Beth, puked in front of the board during her hearing. Sarah, just wanting the meeting to be over, claims to have had black outs since the shooting. Dr. Bowers tells "Beth" that she needs to adjust and accept what happened, but based on "Beth's" statements, Bowers rules her unfit for duty.

characters.name: Arthur (Helena's Child)
characters.description: Arthuris the son ofHelenaandHenrik Johanssen. His twin brother isDonnie. He was originally named Purple, however, Helena decided to name him afterArthur Bell. Arthur was born in theDyad Institute's basement, in the episode "To Right the Wrongs of Many". Helena gives birth to him and his twin brother with the help ofSarah Manningand Arthur Bell.

characters.name: Arthur (Helena's Child)
characters.description: Arthuris the son ofHelenaandHenrik Johanssen. His twin brother isDonnie. He was originally named Purple, however, Helena decided to name him afterArthur Bell. Arthur was born in theDyad Institute's basement, in the episode "To Right the Wrongs of Many". Helena gives birth to him and his twin brother with the help ofSarah Manningand Arthur Bell.

characters.name: Arthur Bell
characters.description: Arthur "Art" Bellis a veteran detective who worked alongsideBeth Childs. After helping his partner get away with a civilian shooting, Art unknowingly meetsSarah Manning, Beth'scloneposing as his partner. Art soon grows suspicious of her uncharacteristic and erratic behavior and begins to suspect not everything is as it seems. One thing becomes clear; he is not a man who likes a mystery to go unsolved. Arthur Bell isBeth Childs's partner. Out of loyalty, he helped clear Beth of a civilian shooting. Art was the first person Beth called after shootingMaggie Chen, a mistake as Beth should have called it in to the station first. Knowing that Beth was "tweaked" on her mixed pills, Art plants the phone in the victim's hand to make it look like Beth thought the woman was holding a gun so that they don't find out about Beth's drugs.

characters.name: Arun Sanghera
characters.description: Vivi Valdez Arun Sangherais a CIA Agent and handler ofVivi Valdez. He also is helpingAlison HendrixandCharlotte Bowlessave theBoston Clonesfrom Davis- the Director of their Clone Program who is determined to let them all get sick with a new virus and die out, so he can close the program. Davis is currently looking for Vivi as she is also a clone, however, she is only beginning to learn about the clone world.

characters.name: Aryanna Giordano
characters.description: Castor clones(genetic brothers)Kendall Malone(sister/genetic template)Kira Manning(genetic niece; viaSarah)Siobhan Sadler(genetic niece; viaKendall) Aryanna Giordanowas a deceasedcloneamong others from Europe whose documentation as well as hair and blood samples were contained in the briefcaseSarahhad retrieved atKatja's hotel. She was a clone of Italian origin. Little is known about her, besides that she is from Rome, Italy.

characters.name: Astrid
characters.description: Astrid was aNeolutioniststrongly affiliated withOlivier Duval. She was constantly by Olivier's side, serving him as an assistant and bodyguard. She was first introduced in "Conditions of Existence" when she accompanied Olivier as he visitedPaul Dierdenand had his back. Later she made another appearance in Club Neolution, where Felix got information from her about Olivier and the Neolutionists. After this event she was seen with Olivier again as she helped him hold Paul captive after his betrayal, and later on also capturedSarah Manningand held her captive in the basement of the club. Eventually she was ambushed byHelenaand put out of commission. Astrid's fate after this is unknown.

Eventually she was ambushed byHelenaand put out of commission. Astrid's fate after this is unknown.

characters.name: Aynsley Norris
characters.description: Aynsley NorriswasAlison Hendrix's nosy suburban neighbor. Although it was never specified how long Aynsley and Alison have known each other, it can be assumed that they have been friends for years. They found refuge in each other, often opening up to each other about their many suburban problems. Aynsley also seems to talk to Alison a lot about their husbands: Aynsley venting to her about her husband,Chad's, affairs and his shortcomings, as well as Donnie, whom Alison constantly defends. Aynsley meetsFelixwhile walking her Great Dane through Scarborough on neighborhood watch. As she arrives at her home on the other side of the street fromAlison's house, she noticesSarah's car parked right in front of her house. She knocks on the window and tellsFelixhe can't be parked there at night. TBA

characters.name: Beth Childs
characters.description: Elizabeth "Beth" Childswas one of the firstclonesseen onOrphan Black. She is a clone of Canadian origin. Her "tag number" is317B31. She becomesSarah'sentry point into the mystery of her suicide as well as the apparent clone conspiracy. Beth is revealed backwards from the moment of her death, as Sarah takes on her identity and reconstructs her last days. By literally becoming Beth, part of Sarah’s journey lies in understanding her, and she comes to deeply respect the valiant, damaged cop she so rashly switched places with. Beth was born on April 1, 1984 in East York, Canada. According to Art, Beth was often seen as somewhat aggressive, headstrong and a bit verbally vulgar. In"Natural Selection", as Beth waited her turn to talk to herdoctor, he mused to Beth "Where's your wit gone? You haven't called me dipshit all day." In "Variation Under Nature", Cosima jokingly advised for Sarah to keep her sense of humor since Beth, evidently, could not. Beth also seemed concerned for and took good care of her physical fitness, together with her boyfriend, Paul. When stressed, Beth tended to ask her doctor for medications. Unfortunately, she tended to mix these up at times and downed them with alcohol, causing constant emotional worry and concern for her boyfriend, Paul.

characters.name: Bobby
characters.description: Bobbyis a sassy bartender at the local pubFelixseems to frequent. She is seen picking on Felix inNatural Selectionfor always giving her a bad attitude. Later, while Felix is hesitating to make a phone call, she snaps a towel at him and tells him to either make a call or hang the phone up. Felix responds in telling her never to snap towels at him, and that he had a very traumatic childhood. InScarred by Many Past Frustrations, she tries to calm and prepareCosima Niehausfor her Sapphire date, advising her not to talk about her ex,Delphine Cormier.

characters.name: Bonnie Johanssen
characters.description: Bonnie Johanssenis the wife ofHenrikand the mother ofGracie. Along with her family, she is a member of theProletheanbrotherhood. In "Governed by Sound Reason and True Religion", Bonnie asked Henrik if she should make another bed for their incoming guest. Henrik told her it would depend on whetherMarkcould get her out of the hospital or not. Later, when the expected "guest",Helena, finally arrived, Bonnie was In "Mingling Its Own Nature With It", her husband is wed to Helena so they could finally inseminate her without violating their religious beliefs.

characters.name: Bulldog
characters.description: Bulldogis a Neolutionist sent to infiltrateTopside. He servedFerdinandas bodyguard while secretly spying on him and sending information toNeolution. In "The Weight of this Combination", Bulldog is under orders by Ferdinand andRachel Duncanto gasAlison Hendrix's family and burn down their house with them as part of a plan by Rachel to assassinate the clones.Sarah Manning, posing as Rachel convinces Ferdinand to cancel the operation and Bulldog then receives a text message to abort while hiding beside a shelf.

characters.name: Cal Morrison
characters.description: "The Great and Unexplored Ocean of Truth"(Audio Series) Cal Morrisonis a recurring character ofOrphan Black. He is an old flame fromSarah'spast and the father of her daughter,Kira. He is a resourceful individualist who walked away from a lucrative tech career when he saw his work co-opted by military contractors because of its potential weapons applications. Now an outdoorsman living comfortably off-the-grid in a house he built in the woods, his hard-won peace is pulled out from under him when Sarah crashes back into his life, bringing her troubles with her.

characters.name: Chad Norris
characters.description: Chad Norrisis the ex-husband ofAynsley Norris. They had two children, and lived on the same street as the Hendrixes. Aynsley and Chad were good friends with Alison and Donnie. One evening, Chad and Aynsley suggested "mixing things up"; at first Donnie and Alison thought in stunned silence they meant an orgy, before awkwardly realizing this was not the case. Instead Chad performed a magic trick, and suggested magic mushrooms. Although Donnie did not (due to sensitivity to them), the three other proceeded to get high. He seems to be submissive to his wife, Aynsley, who was seen often snapping at him in "Variations Under Domestication". Because of their complicated relationship, both had cheated on one another, possibly on more than one occasion. He had an affair with a spin class instructor and also had a sexual encounter with his wife's best friend,Alison Hendrix. Months later, Chad had become depressed. His and Aynsley's two children live at her mother's. He met Alison one day and expressed that he blamed himself for Aynsley's death, that their public sex had driven her to a form of suicide.

characters.name: Character Appearances
characters.description: These charts list the appearances of all characters that have appeared in three or more episodes (extras not included). Unless credited, non-physical appearances such as photographs are not listed. Please use this as a key. The first color reached that applies to the character in the episode is the one used. Character dies in this episode.

characters.name: Charlotte
characters.description: Leda clones(sisters/genetic identicals)Castor clones(genetic brothers)Kendall Malone(sister/original genetic template)Kira Manning(genetic niece; viaSarah)Siobhan Sadler(genetic niece; viaKendall) Charlotte Bowlesis acloneof Project Leda introduced in the finale ofSeason 2. She is an eight-year-old girl who is disabled in one leg. She lived with her adoptive motherMarion Bowlesuntil she was abducted by theNeolutionistsat some unknown point in time duringSeason 3. Charlotte is the sole survivor of over 400 attempts to recreate a clone from the batch of Project Leda. She was created twenty years after the other Leda clones. The original genome the female clones were based on was lost in a fire twenty years before Charlotte was created. Several Project Leda scientists died in the fire, one of whom was thought to beSusan Duncan.Ethan DuncanclaimedAldous Leekieset the fire.

characters.name: Colin
characters.description: "The Great and Unexplored Ocean of Truth"(Audio Series) Colinis the attendant at the morgue whereFelixandVicidentifiedBeth'sbody asSarah. His first appearance was in the series premiere, "Natural Selection", and his latest appearance was in Season 5. When they first met, Colin openly flirted with Felix, despite the strange environment, who eventually caught on and invited him to his home in "Conditions of Existence".

characters.name: Cosima Niehaus
characters.description: Leda clones(sisters/genetic identicals)Castor clones(genetic brothers)Kendall Malone(sister/genetic template)Kira Manning(genetic niece; viaSarah)Siobhan Sadler(genetic niece; viaKendall)Gemma Hendrix(niece; viaAlison)Oscar Hendrix(nephew; viaAlison)Donnie(genetic nephew; viaHelena)Arthur(genetic nephew; viaHelena) *-Cosima Niehausis a Ph.D. student, acloneof American origin and one of the four main protagonists onOrphan Black. Her "tag number" is324B21. She, along with fellow cloneAlison Hendrixfirst meetSarah Manningwhilst the latter is under the guise ofBeth Childs. The three clones decide to group together to form "Clone Club"[4], whose aim is to find their origin and where they come from. Cosima is involved in a romantic relationship withDelphine Cormieruntil they break up due to Cosima believing she could not trust her anymore, but they later get back together. Cosima is currently suffering from an unknown respiratory illness originating from her ovaries and reproductive system which causes her to cough up blood.[5] Cosima is an eccentric, queer, tattooed geek girl with a scientific mind verging on genius. She's dependable, incredibly caring, brave, driven to find answers and, like Sarah, adaptable to extreme circumstances. She also tends to diverge on scientific topics and can talk relatively fast when she gets excited. Cosima's sense of modesty is a little odd. After making love with Delphine the first time, she leaves in just her underwear and coat to run an errand.
